evals.prompt: I'm starting a new TypeScript API project with Express and Postgres. I want to set up my folder structure so Claude doesn't lose context when I switch between planning and writing code. Can you help me organize this?
evals.expected_output: A complete ICM workspace structure with CLAUDE.md, CONTEXT.md files, _config/, and stage directories for a code project archetype

assertions.text: Response includes a visual directory tree structure showing the full workspace layout
Response includes a CLAUDE.md template with a routing table
Response includes CONTEXT.md content for at least 2 individual stages
Response mentions token budgets or context window efficiency
Response includes Layer 3 reference files (_config/ or references/) separate from working directories
Response distinguishes between stable reference material and per-run working artifacts

evals.prompt: I make YouTube videos about AI tools. I need help organizing my research notes, scripts, and production files so Claude can help me at each stage without forgetting my brand voice guidelines
evals.expected_output: A content pipeline workspace with research/, script-lab/, production/, distribution/ stages, voice.md in _config/, and stage contracts with Inputs/Process/Outputs

assertions.text: Response uses content-appropriate stage names (not generic code stages)
Response includes a dedicated voice or brand guidelines file with tone and audience sections
Response uses structured stage contracts with Inputs/Process/Outputs format
Response explains how brand voice stays persistent across stages
Response explains how output from one stage feeds into the next

evals.prompt: My project folder is a mess. I have a huge CLAUDE.md that's like 3 pages long, my reference docs are mixed in with draft outputs, and Claude keeps losing track of what I told it two messages ago. How do I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis of anti-patterns (oversized CLAUDE.md, mixed Layer 3/4), restructuring plan with five-layer hierarchy, and concrete steps to separate factory from product

assertions.text: Response identifies the oversized CLAUDE.md as a problem and recommends a size limit
Response identifies mixing reference docs with draft outputs as a distinct problem
Response explains WHY these problems cause context loss
Response provides a concrete restructuring plan with specific directory structure
Response introduces the concept of separating stable references from working artifacts
